methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $80M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DZYNE Technologies, a Growth-stage defense and security company founded by Matt McCue, has raised $80M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader Defense & Security sector peer set of 10 companies (2 public, 8 private), including comparables such as Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Shield AI, Saronic, and Flock Safety.

Given the early-stage nature of the comp universe and absence of sufficient same-stage, narrow-tier peers, the Peer-Median method was not applicable. Instead, the Stage-Progression method was employed, using the $80M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier to reflect advancement from Growth stage. This yields an implied valuation of $96M. The method assumes standard progression patterns hold for this company's risk and market position.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $82M to $110M, with a median point of $96M. Key uncertainties include the limited precision of a single-method approach, the applicability of standard stage-progression multiples to frontier defense applications, and the absence of tightly comparable private peers at equivalent scale and maturity. Valuation confidence would strengthen with additional revenue or profitability data, customer concentration metrics, and closer-stage peer visibility.
